records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Requited; p. pr. & vb. n. Requiting.]

defs: To repay; in a good sense, to recompense; to return (an equivalent) in good; to reward; in a bad sense, to retaliate; to return (evil) for evil; to punish.
quotes: He can requite thee; for he knows the charma That call fame on such gentle acts as these.
Thou hast seen it; for thou beholdest mischief and spite, to requite it with thy hand.
